video_summary.topic: Opening Range Breakout (ORB) intraday strategy — SPY 5-minute
video_summary.summary: The video describes a complete Opening Range Breakout strategy. The opening range is defined as the high and low of the first 15 minutes after market open (9:30-9:45 ET). Entries trigger on a close above/below the range with volume confirmation. Stops sit at the opposite end of the range; target is 1.5x the range width. All positions are closed by 3 PM.

overall_reason: the video's checkable claim(s) did not hold up against the data

findings.statement: Opening Range Breakout (ORB): enter long when price closes above the first-15-minute high, enter short when price closes below the first-15-minute low. Stop at the opposite end of the opening range. Target 1.5x range size. Exit all positions by 3 PM. Strategy works consistently on SPY / liquid ETFs on a 5-minute chart.
findings.verdict_reason: no edge: profit factor 0.91, net profit -4,082.41 over 66 trades

validations.data_summary: SPY 5m: 66 trades, 48.5% win rate, net -4082.41, max drawdown 6560.31, PF 0.91
validations.result: 32/66 trades won (48.5%); net profit -4082.41; profit factor 0.91
caveats: strategy tester results depend on the instrument, date range, and default capital settings in TradingView
past performance does not predict future results
